jobDistinction.removedLegacyAuditJob: Former broad website issue discovery plus small automatic fixes/upgrades job; removed at user request on 2026-06-01. Do not restore or recreate it.
jobDistinction.currentRemediationJob: Active full-product QA remediation loop (9e36e64a75ae) based on .hermes/remediation-backlog.md; requires real browser/user-path QA, functional side-effect verification, code quality, performance, UX/accessibility, reliability, tests, deployment, and production health checks.

nonOverlapPolicy: If the previous remediation run is still active, skip the current tick instead of starting another fix run.

freshAuditPolicy.action: Run comprehensive audit, update remediation-backlog.md with newly discovered or reprioritized issues, then continue remediation.
freshAuditPolicy.completionAuditDetail: Checklist completion requires a broad audit plus software-tester-style real browser/visual QA on production before a new remediation round starts. Use browser login and click through major pages/workflows visually; split into batches if needed; record/fix/triage findings in remediation-backlog.md before continuing.
completionQaBatches: QA batch 1: login, shell/navigation, dashboard, global search, notifications, preferences/default-page behavior, responsive layout
QA batch 2: VPS/server management and SSH terminal, including Direct Gateway eligibility and idle stability
QA batch 3: Files/storage/SFTP/upload/download/direct access, including sync warnings, capabilities, share links, signed/direct access
QA batch 4: Downloads, Quick Services, Commands, Requests, Deployments, Scheduled Tasks, Templates, Operation Tasks

QA batch 5: Backups/restore, Monitoring, Health/System Health, Settings/admin runtime tuning, Tickets, Announcements/Snippets, API Tokens, AI providers, image bed, API docs, audit/security-adjacent operator flows

lastRun.changeContract: UI/accessibility-only closeout for downloads creation link fields: add explicit htmlFor/id associations and update regression coverage to query by accessible names; do not change download API payloads, task creation semantics, targets, or production data.

evidence.preflight: Recovered dirty worktree containing README plus downloads client/test changes; HEAD matched origin/main; services active.

evidence.production: Build artifacts existed; ownership corrected; vcontrolhub-next and vcontrolhub-ssh-ws restarted; Caddy reloaded; services active; smoke passed 25/25; /api/status 200 with known storage pending-probe warning.
evidence.logs: Recent vcontrolhub-next/vcontrolhub-ssh-ws journal scan found no error/exception/failed/500/hydration/TypeError/ReferenceError matches.
evidence.cleanup: No production download task, QA user, DB row, Docker container, backup artifact, or filesystem canary artifact was created.

lastRun.residual: Continue P2 accessibility/mobile/visual consistency patrol on other placeholder-only or dense controls; downloads create-link label gap is closed.

runPolicy.batchSize: coherent deployable batch; may be larger for high-value architecture/performance/availability/user-experience improvements if fully verified

blockers.reason: Dirty media-library/image-bed navigation slice passed targeted tests, typecheck, lint, and full npm run verify, but production restart/chown activation was blocked by approval policy in the scheduled cron context.
requiredProof: Approve/run the activation command after confirming no next build process is active: chown -R vcontrolhub:vcontrolhub .next dist && systemctl reset-failed vcontrolhub-next.service && systemctl restart vcontrolhub-next.service vcontrolhub-ssh-ws.service.
Run /api/status, ./deploy/smoke-test.sh whrkhldsb.qzz.io vcontrolhub, and recent service log scan after restart.
Only then commit/push the media type switcher and image-bed nav-label dirty slice.

blockers.resolution: Superseded by clean deployed state: commit f666ec7 is on main/origin, backlog records production restart/smoke/log proof for the media/image-bed navigation closure, and this run revalidated /api/status healthy plus smoke 25/25 with clean recent logs. No further restart approval is pending for this blocker.

productDirection: Active full-product canary evidence QA loop with deterministic per-run preflight: every half-hour run must first reconcile environment capabilities, cron health, services, Docker/Quick Services readiness, git/origin state, critical routes, and logs before selecting one real user journey and producing a detailed closeout checklist.

qaControlSystem.purpose: Run VControlHub maintenance as a closed-loop canary QA control system with deterministic environment/capability preflight before every half-hour run, then one concrete real-user journey with evidence matrix and fixes.
qaControlSystem.dirtyWorktreePolicy: If worktree is dirty at start, do not open a new QA path. Classify the diff, validate/deploy/commit/push or safely revert only the current dirty slice, then update state. Dirty worktree alerts are closeout work, not a new feature lane.
qaControlSystem.nonOverlapPolicy: If another remediation/build/test/deploy process is active, skip the tick and record skippedOverlapRuns instead of concurrently editing or deploying.

nextQaPathQueue.path: Login/session -> dashboard shell -> global search -> notifications -> preferences/default page/theme/language/refresh -> logout/login redirect -> responsive/mobile shell

lastEvidenceMatrix.api: Authenticated browser fetches returned /api/system-health 200 with total=16, healthy=15, critical=0 and git-sync healthy at 813273c; /api/health 200 with 3 server health rows; /api/monitoring/stats 200 after deploy with disk="15.7 GB/39.3 GB (40% used)" and topProcesses mem values formatted as percentages.
lastEvidenceMatrix.db_or_persisted_config: Temporary admin qa_cron_monitoring_canary was created only for the canary and cleanup Prisma deleteMany left qa_cron_* remaining=0. No persistent product config was changed.
lastEvidenceMatrix.filesystem_or_sftp: No product storage/SFTP path was intended. Build artifacts .next and dist were regenerated, ownership repaired to vcontrolhub:vcontrolhub, and temporary .hermes helper scripts were removed.
lastEvidenceMatrix.background_task_or_process: Monitoring stats API reads live host /proc/stat, /proc/net/dev, /proc/[pid]/stat and statfs data. The selected side effect exposed a process-table parsing defect in production: before fix the browser showed MEM% values like 3128084M and disk as total/used despite “used” copy; after fix process RSS is bounded against os.totalmem and disk renders used/total.
lastEvidenceMatrix.runtime_or_ui_side_effect: The live Monitoring UI now presents operator-triage resource metrics truthfully: disk usage order matches the used label, and the process table MEM% column is a percent rather than mislabeled raw RSS/page-derived megabytes.
lastEvidenceMatrix.permissions: Unauthenticated preflight still showed guarded /files,/downloads,/operation-tasks 307 and /api/quick-services 401. This canary used an authenticated admin session; health APIs remain protected by health:read/API guard and smoke confirmed API auth blocks unauthenticated requests.
regression: npx vitest run src/app/api/monitoring/stats/__tests__/route.test.ts src/app/api/health/__tests__/route.test.ts src/app/api/system-health/__tests__/route.test.ts --reporter=dot: 2 files / 10 tests passed
lastEvidenceMatrix.production: Repaired .next/dist ownership, restarted vcontrolhub-next.service and vcontrolhub-ssh-ws.service, confirmed vcontrolhub-next/vcontrolhub-ssh-ws/caddy/docker active, and ./deploy/smoke-test.sh whrkhldsb.qzz.io vcontrolhub passed all 24 checks.
lastEvidenceMatrix.logs: Post-restart journal scan over vcontrolhub-next.service and vcontrolhub-ssh-ws.service for the last 10 minutes found no error/warn/failed/exception/EACCES/permission-denied/monitoring/system-health matches.
lastEvidenceMatrix.cleanup: Deleted temporary qa_cron_monitoring_canary (remaining qa_cron_* count 0), removed temporary .hermes helper scripts, left no Quick Services containers/storage artifacts, and prepared state/backlog closeout.

nextQaPathQueue.path: Tickets/Announcements/Snippets/API Tokens/AI providers/Image bed/API docs/Audit -> CRUD/probe/revoke/publish/list permissions -> real side effects and cleanup

lastEvidenceMatrix.browser_or_dom: Production browser reached /login, logged in as qa_cron_integrations_canary, rendered the dashboard shell, and /api-tokens rendered the personal API Token manager form with image:read/image:write scope controls. Browser UI click became stale, so the same CSRF/session-backed production route was exercised deterministically with curl for the side-effect proof.
lastEvidenceMatrix.api: Production /api/login returned 303 with session/CSRF cookies; CSRF POST /api/api-tokens returned 200 and a one-time plaintext token (redacted); Bearer GET /api/images/list returned 200; initial Bearer POST /api/images/upload returned 500 {error:"上传失败"}; after the systemd runtime-path fix, Bearer POST /api/images/upload returned 201 for qa-recanary.png, GET /api/images/[id]/file returned 200 image/png 68 bytes, and CSRF DELETE /api/images/[id] returned 200 success.
lastEvidenceMatrix.db_or_persisted_config: Prisma inspection confirmed qa_cron_integrations_canary existed with admin role, API token scopes [read,image:read,image:write], and no ImageUpload rows after the failing upload. Cleanup deleted the temporary QA user/token and verified remaining qa_cron_* users = 0. The systemd source template now persists writable runtime data paths for future installs/upgrades.
lastEvidenceMatrix.filesystem_or_sftp: Root cause was filesystem/systemd sandboxing: /opt/whrkhldsb/uploads/image-bed existed but was not writable from the vcontrolhub-next.service sandbox because ProtectSystem=strict ReadWritePaths omitted the app upload/runtime data directories. The live upload directory was chowned to vcontrolhub:vcontrolhub, the live unit override added /opt/whrkhldsb/uploads plus app runtime directories, and post-fix upload/delete left no recent files in the image-bed directory.
lastEvidenceMatrix.background_task_or_process: vcontrolhub-next.service runs as vcontrolhub with ProtectSystem=strict. A direct sudo -u vcontrolhub write probe initially failed EACCES, and systemctl show confirmed ReadWritePaths omitted /opt/whrkhldsb/uploads. After daemon-reload and restart, production services were active and the real upload/list/file/delete lifecycle completed through the Next.js process.
lastEvidenceMatrix.runtime_or_ui_side_effect: The visible integration path is now truthful for automation users: an API token with image scopes can perform the image-bed upload workflow end-to-end instead of succeeding at token creation/listing but failing at the real file side effect.
lastEvidenceMatrix.permissions: Unauthenticated preflight remained guarded (/api/quick-services 401 and protected pages 307). Token upload required image:write; list used read/image:read; delete used the authenticated owner session plus CSRF. No broad permission boundary was relaxed.
regression: npx vitest run src/app/api/images/upload/__tests__/route.test.ts src/app/api/images/publish-from-storage/__tests__/route.test.ts src/app/api/images/[id]/file/__tests__/route.test.ts --reporter=dot: 3 files / 13 tests passed
lastEvidenceMatrix.production: Repaired .next/dist/runtime data ownership, added a live systemd ReadWritePaths override for runtime data/upload paths, restarted vcontrolhub-next.service and vcontrolhub-ssh-ws.service, confirmed vcontrolhub-next/vcontrolhub-ssh-ws/caddy/docker active, and ./deploy/smoke-test.sh whrkhldsb.qzz.io vcontrolhub passed all 24 checks.
lastEvidenceMatrix.logs: Recent logs captured the expected pre-fix image-bed:upload errors during the failing canary attempts. After the runtime-path fix, the upload/list/file/delete recanary completed without new image-bed/EACCES failures; final cleanup log scan only showed the earlier pre-fix image-bed:upload lines.
lastEvidenceMatrix.cleanup: Deleted temporary qa_cron_integrations_canary and API token, removed temporary /tmp/vch_int_* and /tmp/vch_recanary_* secret/artifact files, deleted the uploaded test image through the production DELETE route, verified remaining qa_cron_* users = 0, and left no recent files in /opt/whrkhldsb/uploads/image-bed.

nextQaPathQueue.fixSummary: Systemd Next.js unit template now grants runtime uploads/storage/downloads/backups/logs write paths so image-bed uploads can perform their real file side effect under ProtectSystem=strict.

dailyAuditorDirective.reason: 24-hour trend drift: 14 of the last 19 recorded events targeted the same approval-blocked Commands/Operation Tasks dirty closeout. The evidence chain for this slice is already strong, but deployment closeout and canary rotation degraded because the unchanged diff kept rerunning under the same restart-approval blocker.
requiredProof: Before any new QA path, confirm the dirty command diff is unchanged and perform at most one production activation retry for the existing SSH known_hosts fix.

If restart approval is granted, restart vcontrolhub-next.service and vcontrolhub-ssh-ws.service, then run one fresh real command execution canary and capture API/DB/process/log proof that the command still completes and no .ssh/known_hosts read-only write warning remains.
If restart approval is still blocked, record one blocker/skip event plus current service health/origin status, preserve the dirty slice unchanged, and stop without widening scope.
avoidUntilResolved: Do not open a new canary or rotate the queue while src/lib/command/service.ts and its closeout files remain the only intentional dirty diff.
Do not rerun full typecheck/lint/build/runtime-build on an unchanged diff solely to rediscover the same approval blocker unless code changes or runtime ownership drift require it.
Do not treat 307/401/200 liveness probes alone as fresh completion evidence for this slice; activation requires a post-restart real command execution proof chain.
dailyAuditorDirective.resolution: Production activation, smoke, real command canary, clean stderr proof, log scan, and QA cleanup completed for the Commands/Operation Tasks known_hosts slice.

dailyAuditorLastRun.summary: Daily precision audit found strong Change Contract/Evidence Matrix discipline on the selected canaries, but the main loop drifted into repeated approval-blocked dirty-closeout retries on commands-deployments-tasks-canary. Evidence quality and blocker honesty remain good; rotation and deployment closeout need correction.

qaControlSystem.directiveSemantics: If dailyAuditorDirective is active, the main job must satisfy it before normal queue work or write a blocker/skipReason. Directives expire by expiresAfterRuns or when marked resolved by the daily auditor.

examples: minor color/spacing mismatch without readability or workflow impact
non-blocking aria polish unrelated to the selected path
code tidy with no user-path risk or behavior gain
mayFixOnlyIf: directly blocks the selected QA path
breaks keyboard/screen-reader access on the active workflow
is required to verify or safely complete the selected side effect

qaControlSystem.coverageSlo: Every high-priority canary should receive real evidence within a 7-day window unless a higher-priority blocker/directive is recorded.

deterministicPreflight.policy: The main cron must reconcile preflight with state/backlog before selecting a QA path. Stale environment blockers, especially Docker/Quick Services capability drift, must be updated before ordinary work.

lastRunEvaluation.resultSummary: The canary evidence QA system is producing real Change Contract and Evidence Matrix proof on selected runs, but the loop is currently stuck in a repeated approval-blocked dirty closeout for commands-deployments-tasks-canary. Evidence quality and blocker honesty are strong; canary rotation and deployment closeout quality drifted because 14 of the last 19 recent events targeted the same blocked slice.
gaps: The main job is correctly preserving an approval-blocked dirty slice, but 14 of the last 19 recent events were repeated retries on the same commands closeout, so queue rotation stalled.

Because the diff is unchanged and restart approval remains blocked, rerunning typecheck/lint/build/runtime-build on every retry now has low marginal value and violates the lowValueChangeBrake intent unless ownership drift or code changes occur.
The daily precision auditor state itself had not been refreshed since 2026-05-31; this run restores dailyAuditorLastRun/directive bookkeeping so future drift is explicit.
lastRunEvaluation.recommendedNextMode: Honor dailyAuditorDirective closeout-commands-restart-blocker-2026-06-02: treat the next main-job ticks as minimal command closeout-only retries until restart approval is granted or the blocker is explicitly preserved, and do not broaden into a new canary while the dirty slice remains unchanged.

currentCronJobName: VControlHub full-product QA remediation loop — canary evidence QA

commitPolicy.summary: Avoid frequent GitHub commit/push noise. Bundle related remediation changes, verification, deployment proof, backlog/state updates, and roadmap/future-goal updates into one intentional major-round closeout commit whenever safe.
exceptions: blocked/partial verified state where preserving work is safer

checks: major user-path coverage for the round
typecheck/lint/build/runtime build when product code changes

settingsCustomizationDirection.summary: Enrich Settings as the central customization/admin-control surface for safe runtime behavior, UI/personal preferences, notification/channel behavior, polling/list limits, SSH/SFTP/command stability knobs, AI/SMTP/image-bed/API-token/integration options, feature toggles, display/theme/language defaults, and other non-secret settings that currently require code/env edits.
acceptance: each setting has a real runtime side effect
tests at settings API/UI and at least one consumer

changes: major-round closeout must comprehensively check and update GitHub future goals
major-round closeout must update GitHub README.md project goals/roadmap when priorities or completed goals changed

cron.cadenceRationale: User requested less frequent autonomous repair runs; every 30m gives each major round more room for verification/deploy and reduces noise compared with every 20m.

history.summary: QuickService queued lifecycle success messages now expose a direct Task Center link when the API returns taskId, and success/error message regions have status/alert semantics.

lastCompletedSlice.title: QuickService lifecycle task logging observability
lastCompletedSlice.summary: QuickService lifecycle worker now records action-specific progress heartbeats and completion logPreview summaries for Operation Tasks triage, without schema or Docker behavior changes.
lastCompletedSlice.selectedReadmeItem: Docker / QuickService / Direct Gateway 仍有部署边界需要说明和加固（P1）中的 QuickService 生命周期任务日志可观测子切片

evidence: focused worker regression verifies action-specific heartbeat progress
focused worker regression verifies completeJob result.logPreview includes update health/log tail
Operation Tasks service regression remains compatible with logPreview extraction

evidenceMatrix.preflight: Clean git on entry at 73eaa39 matching origin/main; no verify/build/test/deploy overlap; vcontrolhub-next, vcontrolhub-ssh-ws, caddy active; /api/status 200; recent next/ssh-ws logs clean; Docker available; single 6G /swapfile steady.
evidenceMatrix.browser_or_dom: User-facing surface is the existing Operation Tasks progress/logPreview renderer; focused service regressions prove the QuickService lifecycle worker now supplies those fields, and production auth-boundary probes for /operation-tasks and /quick-services returned 307 to login after deployment.
evidenceMatrix.api: No API contract change; QuickService lifecycle APIs continue returning queued durable jobs while the worker enriches existing job progress/result fields consumed by /api/operation-tasks.
evidenceMatrix.db_or_persisted_config: No migration or new table; Job.progress heartbeat values and completeJob result.logPreview carry the new observability payload.
evidenceMatrix.background_task_or_process: Focused worker regression exercised runQuickServiceJobWorkerOnce for quick_service.lifecycle update jobs and verified heartbeat/completeJob calls, including updateService health/logTail in logPreview.
evidenceMatrix.runtime_or_ui_side_effect: Task Center can now show specific lifecycle stages such as preparing update and updating/rebuilding plus a final log preview with health/log tail for QuickService lifecycle jobs.
evidenceMatrix.permissions: No permission boundary changed; lifecycle actions remain behind existing QuickService auth/CSRF route guards and /operation-tasks remains protected.

evidence.summary: Quick Services uninstall now supports in-app confirmation plus optional host data directory cleanup. Default uninstall removes the container and DB record while preserving data; deleteVolumes=true removes only template-recorded /opt/ or /srv/ host mounts and avoids docker socket/timezone/root paths. Additional audit recorded incomplete SFTP media preview Range/share archive boundaries in README.
